tasks.title: Isolate kimi runner into its own plugin for easier runner-specific customization
evidence: DEPENDENCY RQ-0657 COMPLETED: Foundational runner plugin trait and execution framework was created by RQ-0657 (done 2026-02-06T17:19:11Z).

crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/builtin_plugins.rs:527-632: KimiPlugin struct fully implements RunnerPlugin trait with build_run_command, build_resume_command, parse_response_line, and requires_managed_session_id.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/builtin_plugins.rs:590-632: KimiResponseParser implements ResponseParser trait for role=assistant format with content arrays.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/plugin_trait.rs:117-148: RunnerPlugin trait defines the interface for all runner implementations.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/cli_spec.rs:111-121: Kimi CLI option mapping (apply_kimi_options) uses --yolo instead of --approval-mode.
crates/ralph/src/runner/model.rs:31: Kimi default model is 'kimi-for-coding'.
BuiltInRunnerPlugin::Kimi is registered in the plugin enum (builtin_plugins.rs:47) and delegates to KimiPlugin.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:331-362: Legacy run_kimi() function still exists but is now deprecated in favor of KimiPlugin via PluginExecutor.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/builtin_plugins.rs:974-978: Tests verify kimi_requires_managed_session_id behavior.
plan: Verify RQ-0657 plugin framework is complete (it is - marked done).
Review KimiPlugin implementation in builtin_plugins.rs:527-632 for completeness.
Confirm KimiPlugin properly implements all RunnerPlugin trait methods.
Verify KimiResponseParser correctly handles role=assistant format with content arrays.
Ensure cli_spec.rs:apply_kimi_options correctly maps --yolo flag (Kimi doesn't use --approval-mode).
Verify requires_managed_session_id returns true for Kimi (Ralph-managed session IDs).
Run make ci to confirm all tests pass including kimi plugin tests.
Mark task complete - Kimi runner is already isolated into its own plugin via KimiPlugin.
notes: OBSOLETE/REFRESHED: This task was essentially completed as part of RQ-0657 which created the foundational plugin framework and implemented ALL built-in runner plugins including Kimi.
The KimiPlugin struct in builtin_plugins.rs:527-588 fully encapsulates kimi-specific logic:
- build_run_command: Uses --session flag with Ralph-managed session IDs, --print, --prompt
- build_resume_command: Delegates to build_run_command with session_id
- parse_response_line: Uses KimiResponseParser for role=assistant format
- requires_managed_session_id: Returns true (Kimi doesn't emit session IDs in JSON)
The KimiResponseParser in builtin_plugins.rs:590-632 handles Kimi's unique JSON format with role=assistant and content arrays.
Legacy functions in runners.rs (run_kimi, build_kimi_command) are still present but the new code path uses PluginExecutor which delegates to KimiPlugin.
RECOMMENDATION: This task can be marked done after verification, or the scope can be narrowed to 'remove legacy kimi code from runners.rs' if desired.
tasks.request: Isolate the code required for the kimi runner into it's own 'plugin'. Each runner has unique properties and output responses and isolating them will allow me to modify runner specific settings much easier going forward and tailor to each runner. You may keep reusing shared logic in situations where it truly DOES NOT need to be unique to the kimi runner.

custom_fields.status_note: Effectively complete - KimiPlugin implemented in RQ-0657

tasks.title: Add description field to task schema for enhanced context
evidence: User request: Add a new optional description field directly below the title field to allow task builder, task scanner, or manual users to add more detail to context, goal, purpose, desired outcome, etc.
crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs:26-34: Task struct defines the core task schema with title at line 34; description field needs to be added after title.
schemas/queue.schema.json:22-150: Task JSON schema definitions; needs new description field added to Task definition.
crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs: Contains validate_task_required_fields() and other validation logic; needs to make description optional for validation but required for task builder/scanner.
crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_builder.md:34-46: Task builder prompt specifies required and optional keys; must require description for task builder output.
crates/ralph/assets/prompts/scan_*.md: Scanner prompts generate tasks and must require description field in output.
AGENTS.md: Field is optional for task and queue validation to avoid breaking existing queues, but not optional for task builder or scanner.
plan: Add optional description field to Task struct in crates/ralph/src/contracts/task.rs, placed directly after title field (line 34).
Update schemas/queue.schema.json to include description field in Task definition with appropriate type (string) and description.
Update crates/ralph/src/queue/validation.rs to allow description as optional field (no validation errors if missing) to maintain backward compatibility with existing queues.
Update crates/ralph/assets/prompts/task_builder.md: Add description to required keys section, document it as mandatory for task builder output, place directly after title in field ordering.
Update all scanner prompts (scan_maintenance_v1.md, scan_maintenance_v2.md, scan_innovation_v1.md, scan_innovation_v2.md) to require description field in generated tasks.
Ensure description field placement is consistent: directly after title in all schema definitions, struct fields, prompts, and examples.
Run make ci to verify all changes pass formatting, linting, type checking, and tests.
Run ralph queue validate to ensure existing queue files still validate with the new optional field.
notes: Description field requirements: Optional for validation (backward compatibility), mandatory for task builder/scanner (quality/consistency).
Field placement: Must be directly below title field in all schema representations.
Purpose: Allow detailed context, goal, purpose, desired outcome for tasks beyond the short title.
tasks.request: Enhance the task schema to add a new optional description field that is directly below the title. This allows the task builder, task scanner, or manual user created tasks to add more detail to the context, goal, purpose, desired outcome, etc to the task. Update all prompts that interact with the tasks and ensure consistency. Make this field optional for task and queue validation (so existing queues and task dont break validation) but not optional for the task builder, nor the scanner or anything that creates tasks.

tasks.title: Add execution_history.json to RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS to fix run loop aborts
evidence: User report: Run loop aborted after 50 consecutive task failures with error suggesting repo dirty issue
crates/ralph/src/git/clean.rs:25-30: RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS constant missing execution_history.json
RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS currently allows: queue.json, done.json, config.json, productivity.json
Execution history is written to .ralph/cache/execution_history.json during runs (crates/ralph/src/execution_history.rs:83)
When execution_history.json is created/modified, repo becomes 'dirty' causing run failures
Run loop checks repo cleanliness at start using RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS (crates/ralph/src/commands/run/mod.rs:777-781)
Error message: 'Run loop aborted after 50 consecutive task failures. This usually indicates a systemic issue (e.g., repo dirty, runner misconfiguration, or interrupt flag stuck).'
plan: Add '.ralph/cache/execution_history.json' to RALPH_RUN_CLEAN_ALLOWED_PATHS in crates/ralph/src/git/clean.rs
Verify .gitignore already contains .ralph/cache/execution_history.json (it does - line 23)
Add/update test to verify execution_history.json is allowed in repo_dirty_only_allowed_paths check
Verify fix by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass
tasks.request: If the file .ralph/cache/execution_history.json is not in the project specific .gitignore file it is breaking loop runs. Error: Run loop aborted after 50 consecutive task failures. This usually indicates a systemic issue (e.g., repo dirty, runner misconfiguration, or interrupt flag stuck). Check logs above for root cause.

tasks.title: Isolate codex runner into its own plugin for easier runner-specific customization
evidence: User request: Isolate all runners into plugins for easier runner-specific customization.

crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/response.rs:142-154: Codex has unique response parsing (extract_codex_agent_message) for item.completed event type.
crates/ralph/src/runner/execution/runners.rs:62-69: Codex uses legacy_json_format and reasoning_effort options.
AGENTS.md:16-20: Code changes need tests covering success and failure modes.
plan: Wait for RQ-0657 to complete (foundational plugin trait framework).
Create CodexPlugin struct implementing the RunnerPlugin trait.
Implement command building: extract codex-specific logic from runners.rs:run_codex and run_codex_resume, preserving exec/resume subcommands and legacy_json_format.
Implement CLI option mapping: extract codex-specific options from cli_spec.rs:apply_codex_global_options (sandbox modes, bypass approvals).
Implement response parsing: extract codex-specific parsing from response.rs:extract_codex_agent_message for item.completed event type.
Register CodexPlugin as a built-in plugin with appropriate default model.
Refactor main runner execution to delegate to codex plugin when Runner::Codex is selected.
Add comprehensive tests for the codex plugin.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming all tests pass.
tasks.request: create tasks to have all runners isolated and integrated into the plugin system approach. We currently support codex, claude, pi, kimi, opencode, and cursor-agent.

tasks.title: Preserve timeout diagnostics when output-capture mutex is poisoned
evidence: crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:198-205: wrap_output_handler_with_capture drops capture writes if mutex lock fails (including PoisonError).
crates/ralph/src/runutil/execution.rs:299-311: timeout path reads capture via `capture.lock().map(...).unwrap_or_default()`, discarding PoisonError and losing captured output.
Operability impact: timeouts can lose diagnostic output, undermining safeguard dumps.
plan: Handle PoisonError explicitly with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` when locking capture for both writing and reading; log a warning when poison is detected.
Add a unit test that poisons the mutex (panic while holding lock), then invokes the handler and asserts capture still records output and does not panic.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Deduplicate TaskEditKey field formatting to prevent TUI/CLI drift
evidence: crates/ralph/src/tui/task_edit.rs:141-160: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for editing.
crates/ralph/src/queue/operations/edit.rs:630-658: TaskEditKey -> value mapping for preview formatting.
DRY risk: overlapping keys (Request/Duplicates/*At fields) are implemented twice and can drift.
plan: Introduce a shared helper for TaskEditKey field extraction/formatting (in queue module or on Task) and use it from both the TUI editor and the preview code.
Update both call sites to use the shared helper.
Add tests covering a representative Task to ensure both paths render the same values for shared keys.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

evidence: Command: wc -l crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs -> 3347 lines.
AGENTS.md guideline: files >1000 LOC should be split; >800 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Identify cohesive responsibilities inside app.rs (state, actions, event handling, IO, rendering adapters).
Extract into new internal modules under crates/ralph/src/tui/ (e.g., app_state.rs, app_actions.rs, app_events.rs), keeping public surface minimal.
Keep behavior stable by wiring through existing entrypoints and updating imports.
Add/adjust tests for extracted modules where behavior is tricky or under-tested.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.

tasks.title: Warn on .env load errors (ignore NotFound only)
evidence: crates/ralph/src/main.rs:31: `dotenvy::dotenv().ok();` ignores parse/IO errors when a .env is present.
Repro: create an invalid .env in a temp dir and run `ralph --help` from that dir; current code emits no warning, making misconfiguration hard to diagnose.
plan: Change dotenv loading to ignore only NotFound; capture other errors and surface them as a warning (e.g., log after logger init, or print a single redacted warning to stderr).
Add an integration test that runs the ralph binary from a temp dir containing an invalid .env and asserts stderr contains a warning while exit code remains success.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirming it passes.
